title: M/C Journal 'twitter' Issue Now Out!
snippet: Media coverage of the launch of a special 20th-anniversary Twitter issue of the peer-reviewed M/C Journal. Professor Fabio Giglietto is prominently cited as one of the leading, long-standing internet and social media researchers contributing reflections on Twitter's historical legacy and transition into X.
description: Media coverage of the launch of M/C Journal's special 'twitter' issue, highlighting Fabio Giglietto's reflections on the platform's history and its transition to X.

title: Panel – Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research?
snippet: An announcement for the 2026 Social Media & Society conference, listing Fabio Giglietto as a roundtable panelist. The panel convenes leading researchers to discuss their first-hand experiences, challenges, and workarounds using the Meta Content Library (MCL).
description: An announcement for the #SMSociety conference panel featuring Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert speaker discussing the Meta Content Library and the future of social media research.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino
description: News article from Il Resto del Carlino

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: This news article covers the public and political debate triggered by Fabio Giglietto's research paper, 'A Pretty Blunt Approach: Meta's Political Content Reduction Policy and Italian Parliamentarians' Facebook Visibility.' The piece details how Giglietto's findings on algorithmic bias prompted an official inquiry at the European Parliament.
description: An article discussing the political and public fallout of Professor Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithms, including its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.

title: La schedatura dei docenti, l’antifascismo e il tarlo della scuola del ’68 - Centro Studi Sereno Regis

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: A detailed media feature covering Fabio Giglietto's investigation of 2.5 million Facebook posts. The news piece quotes Giglietto and explains how Facebook's algorithm penalizes official institutional politics and boosts extremist, euroskeptic, and anti-European voices, sparking talks for bipartisan platform regulation in the Italian Parliament.
description: A prominent news feature on Open.online detailing Fabio Giglietto's study on how Facebook's algorithm penalizes official political accounts while boosting extremist, euroskeptic content.
